comments.content:
* CodeDisplay.astro
 * A clean, professional code display component for showcasing code snippets.
 * 
 * Features:
 * - Syntax highlighting
 * - Copy to clipboard functionality
 * - Optional line numbers
 * - Expandable view
 * - Multiple themes
 * - Terminal mode option

comments.content:
* EnhancedWindows95Window.astro
 * An enhanced Windows 95 window component with better resizing, animations, and z-index management.
 * 
 * Features:
 * - Smooth animations for opening, closing, minimizing, and maximizing
 * - Proper resize handles with constraints
 * - Better z-index management for window stacking
 * - Window snapping to screen edges and other windows
 * - Keyboard shortcuts for window management
comments.name: Component

comments.content:
* ParticleBackground.astro
 *
 * A robust particle background component that works with particles.js
 * and has proper error handling with CSS fallback.
 *
 * Features:
 * - Uses NosytLabs brand colors (purple and orange)
 * - Has fallback when particles.js fails to load
 * - Supports reduced motion preferences
 * - Customizable via props
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* ID for the particle container
   * @default "particle-background"
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Primary color for particles
   * @default "rgba(124, 58, 237, 0.5)" (Purple)
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Secondary color for particles (used in gradient mode)
   * @default "rgba(255, 107, 0, 0.5)" (Orange)
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Number of particles to display
   * @default 100
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Size of particles in pixels
   * @default 3
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:

   * @default 1
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:

   * @default true
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:

   * @default true

comments.name: unknown

   * @default 0

 * - Fallback for older browsers
 * - Proper accessibility attributes

comments.content:
* Knowledge Graph Utility
 * 
 * This utility provides functions to retrieve and format content
 * from the Knowledge Graph for use in the website.
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Fetches all entities of a specific type from the Knowledge Graph
 * @param {string} entityType - The type of entities to fetch (e.g., 'Project', 'Service')
 * @returns {Promise<Array>} - Array of entities
comments.name: getEntitiesByType

comments.content:
* Fetches an entity by name from the Knowledge Graph
 * @param {string} entityName - The name of the entity to fetch
 * @returns {Promise<Object|null>} - The entity object or null if not found
comments.name: getEntityByName

comments.content:
* Fetches all entities related to a specific entity from the Knowledge Graph
 * @param {string} entityName - The name of the entity to find relations for
 * @param {string} relationType - Optional relation type to filter by
 * @returns {Promise<Array>} - Array of related entities
comments.name: getRelatedEntities

comments.content:
* Formats entity observations into HTML content
 * @param {Object} entity - The entity object with observations
 * @returns {string} - HTML content
comments.name: formatEntityContent

comments.content:
* YouTube Scraper Utility
 *
 * This utility provides functions to fetch YouTube data via web scraping
 * rather than using the YouTube API. This approach is preferred for this project
 * as mentioned in the requirements.
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Fetches YouTube channel data by scraping the channel page
 * @param {string} channelId - The YouTube channel ID or handle (e.g., '@TycenYT')
 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Channel data including title, description, subscribers, etc.
comments.name: fetchChannelData

comments.content:
* Fetches recent videos from a YouTube channel by scraping
 * @param {string} channelId - The YouTube channel ID or handle (e.g., '@TycenYT')
 * @param {number} limit - Maximum number of videos to return
 * @returns {Promise<Array>} - Array of video objects
comments.name: fetchChannelVideos

comments.content:
* Fetches data for a specific YouTube video by scraping
 * @param {string} videoId - The YouTube video ID
 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Video data
comments.name: fetchVideoData

* Creates an embed URL for a YouTube video with custom parameters
 * @param {string} videoId - The YouTube video ID
 * @param {Object} options - Embed options
 * @returns {string} - The embed URL
comments.name: createEmbedUrl

comments.content:
* @fileoverview NosytOS95 Easter Eggs and Hidden Features
 *
 * This module contains various Easter eggs and hidden features for the NosytOS95 interface.
 * Features include Konami code activation, secret click combinations, keyboard shortcuts,
 * and hidden messages. All Easter eggs are designed to enhance user experience without
 * interfering with normal functionality.
 *
 * Features:
 * - Konami code (↑↑↓↓←→←→BA) for retro game effects
 * - Secret click combinations on UI elements
 * - Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Alt+N, Ctrl+Alt+D)
 * - Hidden console messages on icon hover
 * - Animated visual effects and sound integration
 *
 * @module easter-eggs
 * @version 1.0.0
 * @author NosytLabs Team
 * @since 1.0.0
comments.name: unknown

comments.content:
* Initializes all Easter egg functionality for the NosytOS95 interface
 *
 * Sets up event listeners and handlers for various Easter egg triggers
 * including Konami code, secret clicks, keyboard shortcuts, and hidden messages.
 * This function should be called once when the DOM is ready.
 *
 * @returns {void}
 * @example
 * // Initialize Easter eggs on DOM ready
 * document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
 *   initEasterEggs();
 * });
 *
 * @since 1.0.0
comments.name: initEasterEggs

comments.content:
* Set up the Konami code Easter egg
 * Up, Up, Down, Down, Left, Right, Left, Right, B, A
comments.name: setupKonamiCode

comments.content: * Activate the Konami code Easter egg
comments.name: activateKonamiCode

comments.content: * Set up secret clicks Easter eggs
comments.name: setupSecretClicks

comments.content: * Activate the clock Easter egg
comments.name: activateClockEasterEgg

comments.content: * Activate the logo Easter egg
comments.name: activateLogoEasterEgg

comments.content: * Set up keyboard shortcuts Easter eggs
comments.name: setupKeyboardShortcuts

comments.content: * Activate Nosyt mode Easter egg
comments.name: activateNosytMode

comments.content:
* Activates developer mode Easter egg with animated code snippets
 *
 * Creates floating code elements across the desktop with random rotation
 * and positioning. Plays a special sound effect and displays a message.
 * The effect automatically removes itself after 8 seconds.
 *
 * @returns {void}
 * @example
 * // Activate developer mode (usually triggered by secret key combination)
 * activateDeveloperMode();
 *
 * @since 1.0.0
comments.name: activateDeveloperMode

comments.content:
* Generates a random code snippet for developer mode display
 *
 * Returns a random JavaScript code snippet from a predefined array
 * of NosytOS-related code examples for visual effect.
 *
 * @returns {string} Random code snippet string
 * @example
 * const snippet = getRandomCodeSnippet();
 * console.log(snippet); // "function initNosytOS() { /* ...
comments.name: unknown

comments.content: * Set up hidden messages Easter eggs
comments.name: setupHiddenMessages

comments.content:
* Retrieves a secret message for a specific desktop icon
 *
 * Returns a predefined humorous or cryptic message associated with
 * the given icon name. Used for hidden console messages when hovering
 * over desktop icons.
 *
 * @param {string} iconName - The name/label of the desktop icon
 * @returns {string} Secret message string for the icon
 * @example
 * const message = getSecretMessageForIcon('Notepad');
 * console.log(message); // "All work and no play makes Nosyt a dull boy."
 *
 * @example
 * const message = getSecretMessageForIcon('Unknown Icon');
 * console.log(message); // "Nosyt was here."
 *
 * @since 1.0.0
comments.name: getSecretMessageForIcon

comments.content:
* Displays an Easter egg message overlay on the desktop
 *
 * Creates a styled message element with title and content that appears
 * on the desktop and automatically removes itself after 4 seconds with
 * a fade-out animation.
 *
 * @param {string} title - The title text for the message
 * @param {string} message - The main message content
 * @returns {void}
 * @example
 * showEasterEggMessage('DEVELOPER MODE', 'You\'ve unlocked the matrix!');
 *
 * @example
 * showEasterEggMessage('Secret Found', 'You discovered a hidden feature!');
 *
 * @since 1.0.0
comments.name: showEasterEggMessage

* @fileoverview JSDoc Standardization Script
 * 
 * This script analyzes existing JSDoc comments across the codebase
 * and provides recommendations for standardization according to
 * NosytLabs documentation standards.
 * 
 * @module standardize-jsdoc
 * @version 1.0.0
 * @author NosytLabs Team
 * @since 1.0.0
comments.name: unknown
unknown
unknown

 * @param {string} dir - Directory to search

 * @returns {string[]} Array of file paths

 * @param {string} content - File content to analyze
 * @returns {object[]} Array of JSDoc comment objects
comments.name: extractJSDocComments

 * @returns {object} File analysis results
comments.name: analyzeFile

 * @param {object[]} fileResults - Array of file analysis results
 * @returns {string[]} Array of recommendation strings
comments.name: generateRecommendations
